2605.07138.title: Can You Break RLVER? Probing Adversarial Robustness of RL-Trained Empathetic Agents
2605.07138.paper_summary: Reinforcement learning from verifiable emotion rewards RLVER has produced language models with strong empathetic performance, evaluated on benchmarks that assume cooperative, honest users. Yet real emotional interactions systematically violate this assumption: users gaslight, escalate, and pressure AI systems for unconditional validation, dynamics that cooperative benchmarks cannot surface. We construct the Adversarial Empathy Benchmark AEB and introduce the Emotional Consistency Score ECS to evaluate empathetic robustness under adversarial conditions. AEB comprises six psychologically grounded adversarial trajectory types with discriminative reward structures that penalize formulaic responses; ECS formally disentangles a model's capacity to track user emotional states from its capacity to improve them. In a controlled experiment across eight scenario-matched conditions (think and no-think conditions on 2 RLVER models, and 2 base models (Qwen 1.5B and 7B) with 480 adversarial dialogues), RLVER-PPO-Think substantially outperforms the same-scale untuned baseline (0.963 vs. 0.761, \(p<0.001, r=0.688\)), with zero dialogue collapses and 47\% higher hidden-intention detection. However, ECS remains nearly flat and is not significantly different for RLVER-PPO-Think versus Base-7B-Think (\(p=0.650\)): RL training improves emotional responsiveness without measurable gains in observable state tracking. We interpret the ECS--FS (Final Score) gap as a behavioral/legibility dissociation inside this simulator family, not as evidence about internal understanding or clinical readiness.
2605.07138.paper_summary_zh: 強化學習從可驗證情感獎勵（RLVER）已經產生了在假設合作、誠實用戶的基準上表現出強大同理心的語言模型。<lb/>然而，真實的情感互動系統性地違反了這一假設：用戶對 AI 系統進行心理操控、升級和施壓以獲得無條件的認可，這些動態是合作基準無法揭示的。<lb/>我們構建了對抗性同理心基準（AEB），並引入情感一致性分數（ECS）來評估在對抗條件下的同理心穩健性。<lb/>AEB 包含六種心理學基礎的對抗性軌跡類型，具有懲罰公式化反應的區別性獎勵結構；ECS 正式區分模型追蹤用戶情感狀態的能力與改善它們的能力。<lb/>在一個控制實驗中，針對八個場景匹配條件（對 2 個 RLVER 模型的思考和不思考條件，以及 2 個基礎模型（Qwen 1.5B 和 7B）進行了 480 次對抗性對話），RLVER-PPO-Think 的表現顯著優於同規模的未調整基線（0.963 對 0.761，\(p<0.001, r=0.688\)），且沒有對話崩潰，隱藏意圖檢測提高了 47%。<lb/>然而，ECS 仍然幾乎保持平坦，且 RLVER-PPO-Think 與 Base-7B-Think 之間沒有顯著差異（\(p=0.650\)）：RL 訓練提高了情感反應能力，但在可觀察狀態追蹤上沒有可測量的增益。<lb/>我們將 ECS--FS（最終分數）差距解釋為這個模擬器家族內部的行為/可讀性分離，而不是對內部理解或臨床準備的證據。
2605.07138.author: Deeraj S K et.al.
2605.07138.authors: Deeraj S K, Sadhana Devarajan, Krishna Mehra, Sudhakar Mishra
